title: Fix HP preservation on character select and attack tick rate defaults

requirements.text: When a player backs out to the character select screen and re-selects their character, the character's current HP must be preserved exactly as it was — it must NOT reset to max HP. The HP value should be saved (either to backend or persistent local state) before navigating away from the dungeon/game screen and restored when re-entering with that character.

quotes: If I back out of the game to the character select screen my characters life is still going back to 20/max no matter how much life I had before going back to the character select screen. This needs to be fixed.
It should preserve whatever HP the character was at.

acceptanceCriteria: Player enters a dungeon run with, e.g., 45/100 HP
Player backs out to the character select screen
The character's HP displays as 45/100, not 100/100 or 20/max
HP preservation works consistently across multiple back-and-forth navigations

requirements.text: Fix the player attack tick rate so the player attacks every 12 ticks by default (not every tick). Additionally, set level 1 enemies to attack every 21 ticks, scaling down by 1 tick every 5 enemy levels (level 5 = 20 ticks, level 10 = 19 ticks, level 15 = 18 ticks, etc.). These values must be defined as named constants in the combat engine and must not be changed unless explicitly requested.

quotes: I'm attacking every tick. I thought we had this set to 12 ticks.
Make player default 12 ticks. Make level 1 enemy 21 ticks. Scale 1 lower on ticks for enemies every 5 levels.

acceptanceCriteria: Player character attacks exactly once every 12 combat ticks
A level 1 enemy attacks once every 21 ticks
A level 5 enemy attacks once every 20 ticks
A level 10 enemy attacks once every 19 ticks
Tick scaling formula is: enemyAttackTicks = 21 - Math.floor((enemyLevel - 1) / 5)
Player attack interval constant is named clearly (e.g., PLAYER_ATTACK_INTERVAL = 12) in combatEngine.ts
No other combat logic or game behavior is changed

constraints: Do not change any game logic, features, or mechanics outside of HP preservation and attack tick rate
Do not alter EXP scaling or any other combat parameters
Do not modify immutable frontend paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, hooks/useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui)
nonGoals: EXP scaling based on monster level and stats (deferred by user)
Any other combat rebalancing or new features
